# JS Agent → Full-Fledge Agent: Enhancement Plan

This plan compares the JS Agent's current capabilities against VS Raptor's architecture and identifies the gaps. Items are grouped by priority.

---

## Gap Analysis: What VS Raptor Has That JS Agent Lacks

| Capability | VS Raptor | JS Agent |
|---|---|---|
| Parallel sub-agent spawning | `spawnAgent` tool | None |
| LSP / semantic code nav | `lsp` tool (definition, refs, hover, symbols) | None |
| Workspace diagnostics | `getDiagnostics` | None |
| Structured todo management | `todoWrite` persisted to file | `todo_write` exists but localStorage only |
| Scoped persistent memory | `memoryRead/Write` with `global` + `project` scopes | `memory_write/search/list` single-scope only |
| File editing (surgical) | `editFile` with `oldString→newString` | `file_write` only (full overwrite) |
| Multi-file atomic edits | `multiEdit` (validates all before writing) | None |
| Pre-edit read enforcement | Explicit rule: must read before editing | No enforcement |
| Verified execution | Runs code, checks output, reports faithfully | `runtime_runTerminal` exists but no verification loop |
| Blast-radius reasoning | Explicit risky-action confirmation rules | `steerToolCall` blocks catastrophic commands only |
| Context auto-summarization | Built into the IDE loop | Exists, but only LLM-driven (no fallback digest) |
| Tool dependency sequencing | Parallel calls only when independent | Parallelizes all read-only tools; no dependency graph |
| Real file-type awareness | Reads, diffs, and edits with language-aware context | Raw file I/O only |

---

## Enhancement Plan (Prioritized)

### P0 — Core Agentic Loop Upgrades

#### 1. Sub-Agent Spawning (`worker_spawn`)
The single biggest gap. Raptor uses `spawnAgent` to delegate bounded sub-tasks to independent loops. JS Agent has `worker_batch` / `worker_list` but no true nested loop delegation.

**Plan:**
- Add a `worker_spawn` tool that forks a new bounded agent loop with its own message history, tool set, and round limit
- Parent loop waits for child result or times out and continues
- Child reports back as a structured `tool_result` block
- Use `BroadcastChannel` (already wired for cache sync) to relay child status to parent UI

#### 2. Surgical File Editing (`file_edit` with patch semantics)
Currently `file_write` overwrites the entire file. This is destructive and causes data loss on partial updates.

**Plan:**
- Add `file_edit` tool: takes `path`, `oldString`, `newString`, optional `replaceAll`
- Enforce uniqueness check: if `oldString` matches 0 or 2+ locations, return error instead of guessing
- Add `file_multi_edit`: array of `{path, oldString, newString}` operations validated atomically before any write
- Log a pre-edit diff preview into the tool result for transparency

#### 3. Tool Dependency Graph & Verified Execution
Currently the loop parallelizes all read-only tools without checking data dependencies. It also never re-verifies that a written file compiles, tests pass, or a command succeeded.

**Plan:**
- Add dependency tags to tool metadata: `reads: [paths]`, `writes: [paths]`
- Loop scheduler checks write/read intersections before batching — tools with overlapping paths run sequentially
- After any `file_write` / `file_edit`, auto-enqueue a lightweight verification step (syntax check or test run) and surface the result before the next LLM call

---

### P1 — Intelligence & Memory Upgrades

#### 4. Scoped

# JS Agent → Full-Fledge Agent: Enhancement Plan

This plan compares the JS Agent's current capabilities against VS Raptor's architecture and identifies the gaps. Items are grouped by priority.

---

## Gap Analysis: What VS Raptor Has That JS Agent Lacks

| Capability | VS Raptor | JS Agent |
|---|---|---|
| Parallel sub-agent spawning | `spawnAgent` tool | None |
| LSP / semantic code nav | `lsp` tool (definition, refs, hover, symbols) | None |
| Workspace diagnostics | `getDiagnostics` | None |
| Structured todo management | `todoWrite` persisted to file | `todo_write` exists but localStorage only |
| Scoped persistent memory | `memoryRead/Write` with `global` + `project` scopes | `memory_write/search/list` single-scope only |
| File editing (surgical) | `editFile` with `oldString→newString` | `file_write` only (full overwrite) |
| Multi-file atomic edits | `multiEdit` (validates all before writing) | None |
| Pre-edit read enforcement | Explicit rule: must read before editing | No enforcement |
| Verified execution | Runs code, checks output, reports faithfully | `runtime_runTerminal` exists but no verification loop |
| Blast-radius reasoning | Explicit risky-action confirmation rules | `steerToolCall` blocks catastrophic commands only |
| Context auto-summarization | Built into the IDE loop | Exists, but only LLM-driven (no fallback digest) |
| Tool dependency sequencing | Parallel calls only when independent | Parallelizes all read-only tools; no dependency graph |
| Real file-type awareness | Reads, diffs, and edits with language-aware context | Raw file I/O only |

---

## Enhancement Plan (Prioritized)

### P0 — Core Agentic Loop Upgrades

#### 1. Sub-Agent Spawning (`worker_spawn`)
The single biggest gap. Raptor uses `spawnAgent` to delegate bounded sub-tasks to independent loops. JS Agent has `worker_batch` / `worker_list` but no true nested loop delegation.

**Plan:**
- Add a `worker_spawn` tool that forks a new bounded agent loop with its own message history, tool set, and round limit
- Parent loop waits for child result or times out and continues
- Child reports back as a structured `tool_result` block
- Use `BroadcastChannel` (already wired for cache sync) to relay child status to parent UI

#### 2. Surgical File Editing (`file_edit` with patch semantics)
Currently `file_write` overwrites the entire file. This is destructive and causes data loss on partial updates.

**Plan:**
- Add `file_edit` tool: takes `path`, `oldString`, `newString`, optional `replaceAll`
- Enforce uniqueness check: if `oldString` matches 0 or 2+ locations, return error instead of guessing
- Add `file_multi_edit`: array of `{path, oldString, newString}` operations validated atomically before any write
- Log a pre-edit diff preview into the tool result for transparency

#### 3. Tool Dependency Graph & Verified Execution
Currently the loop parallelizes all read-only tools without checking data dependencies. It also never re-verifies that a written file compiles, tests pass, or a command succeeded.

**Plan:**
- Add dependency tags to tool metadata: `reads: [paths]`, `writes: [paths]`
- Loop scheduler checks write/read intersections before batching — tools with overlapping paths run sequentially
- After any `file_write` / `file_edit`, auto-enqueue a lightweight verification step (syntax check or test run) and surface the result before the next LLM call

---

### P1 — Intelligence & Memory Upgrades

#### 4. Scoped Persistent Memory (`global` vs `project` vs `session`)
Current memory is single-scope (localStorage). Raptor separates:
- `global` — user preferences, cross-project patterns
- `project` — architecture facts, build commands, known issues
- `session` — turn-level context (already exists as `context_summary`)

**Plan:**
- Extend `memory_write` / `memory_search` to accept a `scope` field: `global | project | session`
- `global` scope persists under a user-wide key independent of the current working directory
- `project` scope keys off `fs_list_roots()` to derive a project identifier
- Surface scope in `memory_list` output and inject project-scope memory into system prompt automatically at turn start

#### 5. Structured Todo / Task Tracking (File-Backed)
Current `todo_write` stores only in localStorage — ephemeral across devices, lost on clear. Raptor persists todos to `.raptor-todos.json` in the workspace.

**Plan:**
- Add file-backed todo store: write/read `.agent-todos.json` in the active project root
- Mark todos `pending → in_progress → completed` with timestamps
- Auto-inject active `in_progress` todos into the runtime continuation prompt so the model never loses task context across compaction

#### 6. Intent-to-Plan Upgrade (Multi-Step Decomposition)
The current preflight generates a `query_plan`
